item.title: Finally finished this. So many problems.
item.description:
Our big laser is alive again!! For 3 MONTHS we couldn't get her going. Was pulling my hair out. This is the first piece we were able to make after the 3 months of down-time. If your a laser person, you understand how it can go... Thanks for looking. "Demure" 48" Round 12 Layers Hangs on a French Cleat Took 1 Billion hours to design and make (Approximation)
I also find "Demure" extremely difficult to photograph. Just the photo took me forever to get/edit. But, I'm happy with it.
Thanks for looking. If you want to see other things I make, check it out here: philip-roberts.com/ No cat tax. We have feral cats around the art studio that are cute and they keep their distance. I just found one in the nearest road and had to burry it about 20 minutes ago. Not a great morning.
item.dc:creator: PhilipRobertss

item.title: Thanks NPR
item.description: Specifically from this website. https://www.plancpills.org
item.dc:creator: MilkMenace

item.title: Went to Planned Parenthood for the yearly
item.description:
And man. I just cannot underscore how amazing it was. Got a mammogram scheduled, got an IUD inserted, got an appointment with a menopause doctor. Issues I’ve been discussing for years with my GP were addressed THEN. Like, at the appointment. Immediately. I had no idea IUD was an option for me. I had no idea there were doctors who focus on peri menopause & menopause. I WISH my regular doctor were even half as interested in my well being as this doctor was. AND There’s a literal Plan B vending machine outside. Chef’s kiss.
I first went to PP when I was 15. They were my hero then, and 32 years later they still rock. In closing: Got reproductive organs? Even old ones? Go to Planned Parenthood if it’s an option in your area. They bill insurance, give excellent care, & you get to support something important. #plannedparenthood #abortionishealthcare #fuckyouscotus
item.dc:creator: Mbcb350

item.title: Happy Dog Gets a Love-filled Sendoff After Spending 6 Years at a Shelter
item.description: There's no better example of someone getting what they've long deserved than this sweet shelter dog named Stevie. This adorable pup—with a shiny black coat and white markings on his chest—spent almost six years at Dogtown South Africa before finally meeting his forever family. If that's not enough to make your heart melt, watching his joyous send off certainly will. The sanctuary for dogs who've lost their families or were rescued from dire situations posted the heartwarming video to its TikTok account (dogtownsa) June 23 showing Stevie's "walk of honor." It shows Stevie being led through a lineup of Dogtown team members who one-by-one shower him with goodbye pats and cuddles before he jumps onto the tailgate of a truck, presumably to head to his new home. It sure seems like Steve is thanking his caretakers for the past six years as the shelter staff applauds.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Malicious compliance
item.description:
https://www.chron.com/news/houston-texas/article/Pregnant-Texas-woman-driving-in-HOV-lane-told-17293221.php She got the ticket, and is planning on fighting it. This is exactly the sort of tedious nitpicking legal battles people should be fighting, because it establishes legal precedent.

item.title: Green derecho storm over South Dakota.
item.description: The green color is due to red-yellow sunlight mixing with the blue light from the rain, hail, and water in the storm, This phenomena only occurs in very large thunderstorms when the light has sufficient depth to fully mix and become green. Shelf clouds form on the leading edge of storms where air is getting sucked into the convective core. The backside of shelf clouds is colloquially called the whales mouth.
item.dc:creator: dolphinyaso
